title: The Great Kelp Caper of the Pink Continent
summary: When Rainbow Mer-Girl discovers her backyard kelp forest has been devoured, she follows a trail of crumbs from the Big Pink Continent all the way to the Big Orange Continent of Australia. There, she confronts the hungry Kelp Bass responsible for the theft. Just as the underwater residents worry about their remaining greenery, the heroic Super Kitten arrives with a solution: starting a communal kelp farm. By planting rows of seedlings, the group ensures that everyone has enough to eat, protecting the local backyards from future raids.

highlightQuote.text: Well, we live in a gigantic Pink Continent, so there's Kelp Bass around there. And it's so hard to see you because we have to drive a long ways here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There's the stars. We're all set up for stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Rainbow Mer-Girl. Can this be one about Pink Mer-Girl and Rainbow Mer-Girl?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: About Pink Mer-Girl and Rainbow Mer-Girl? Sure.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, so, you're Pink Mer-Girl, I'm Rainbow Mer-Girl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, that sounds good. Ding dong! Who is it? said Pink Mer-Girl.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Rainbow Mer-Girl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi Rainbow Mer-Girl. What are you up to today?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I have a kelp forest in my backyard, but today it's gone!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's gone? What happened to it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. I saw a big shadow out my window last night eating my kelp forest but it escaped when I went out the back door to check on my kelp forest.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A big shadow? I wonder what it was. Who do you think could help us figure it out?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's ask Mama and Papa Mer-Girl. Mama and Papa Mer-Girl!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yes, what's up?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Rainbow Mer-Girl doesn't know where her kelp forest is and last night she went out to check on her kelp forest and all her kelp forest was gone because she saw a big shadow eating her kelp forest. And now there's so many crumbs across her yard!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Crumbs? What kind of crumbs?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Kelp crumbs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mama and Papa Mer-Girl said, Well, that is very alarming, because we also have a kelp forest. I hope no one eats ours. But what could that big shadow have been? What kind of creatures do you think would make a big shadow like that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Could it have been a seahorse or a crab?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, those are just tiny animals.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Could it have been... a shark?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cuz I heard the shark alarm, says Rainbow Mer-Girl. No, I have a shark alarm! Cuz I heard my shark alarm last night, so I had to check on my kelp forest.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But do sharks eat kelp?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What kind of shark is that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, no. It was Kelp Bass. They ate the kelp. So my Kelp Bass alarm rang.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Kelp Bass? We don't usually have Kelp Bass around these parts.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, we live in a gigantic Pink Continent, so there's Kelp Bass around there. And it's so hard to see you because we have to drive a long ways here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We do? Where are we driving to?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I just had to drive from a Big Pink Continent, which is very far.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, I think we better go to the Kelp Bass's house and sort this out. Do you know where the Kelp Bass lives?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Follow me! Swim, swim, swim. And they swam to a Big Orange Continent. This is called Australia. Kelp Bass live around here somewhere. Oh, that green bass is them!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hello!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Have you eaten my kelp forest? says Rainbow Mer-Girl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I don't know, we just eat kelp wherever we can find it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I saw a shadow that had fins, it was green, and it was a Kelp Bass. I know it was a Kelp Bass! And my Kelp Bass alarm went off so I had to check on my kelp forest, but it was gone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh dear. Well, what could we do about that? You know, we just love eating kelp, but we don't always know if it belongs to somebody else.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Maybe if you planted something else in your yard, then we wouldn't eat it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, all we like to nibble on is kelp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You like kelp too, I see. How could we solve this problem then?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Super Kitten to the rescue! Meow, meow!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you must be Super Kitten. How can you help us out?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Meow, meow, meow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A kelp farm? What's a farm?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Meow, meow, meow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see, that's an interesting approach. That just might work. I think you might have solved all our problems. You see, the idea is to plant our own kelp farm so we'll have more kelp than we'd ever want to eat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Would you help us figure out how to plant our first kelp farm, Mer-Girls? And what about you, Super Kitty?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Meow, meow! And I also brought a little fish snack for the Mer-Girls.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks! Nom nom nom. They ate it all up.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then together, they planted row after row of tiny kelp seedlings so that the Kelp Bass would always have as much kelp as they could ever eat. And then they didn't have to go around eating the kelp forests in the Mer-Girls' backyards. And that's the end of our story.

characters.name: Rainbow Mergirl
characters.description: A resourceful mergirl known for her clever use of kelp to aid the Lanternshark, she balances her practical ingenuity with a spirited streak, recently exemplified by a sharp disagreement with the Pink Mergirl over their cherished stuffed animals.

characters.name: Pink Mer-Girl
characters.description: Rainbow Mer-Girl's friend who joins the search for the kelp thief.

characters.name: Mama Mer-Girl
characters.description: A mermaid parent who worries about the safety of her own kelp forest.

characters.name: Papa Mer-Girl
characters.description: A mermaid parent who helps the girls brainstorm what kind of creature could eat a whole forest.

characters.name: Kelp Bass
characters.description: A green, finned fish living near Australia with a massive appetite for kelp.

characters.name: Super Kitten
characters.description: A heroic feline who speaks in meows and proposes building a sustainable kelp farm.

places.name: Backyard
places.description: Behind Hattie’s workshop and Uncle Griscko’s house, an eclectic sprawl of junk litters a landscape once home to a lush kelp forest, long since devoured by a mysterious shadow. A towering spaceship now looms over the site’s inaugural breach—a raw, jagged excavation where the earth was first struck and the bitten pool noodle was originally unearthed.

places.name: Big Pink Continent
places.description: The faraway home of the Mer-Girls where Kelp Bass are common.

places.name: Australia
places.description: A remote country spanning the Big Orange Continent, characterized by the winding labyrinth of the gopher’s tunnel and the amber-lit coastal waters where the Kelp Bass thrive.

places.name: Kelp Farm
places.description: A newly planted agricultural site designed to provide unlimited food for sea creatures.
